message.content: How do I identify which GPU is installed on a Linux system?

content.text:
Use lspci to list all PCI devices. To filter for the GPU specifically, run: lspci | grep -i vga

This returns the GPU model name and PCI address. For example, on an AMD system it might return something like:

01:00.0 VGA compatible controller: Advanced Micro Devices, Inc. [AMD/ATI] Navi 21 [Radeon RX 6800 XT]

The lspci command is part of the pciutils package, available on all major Linux distributions.

message.content: What if I want more detail about the driver in use?

content.text:
Add the -v flag: lspci -v | grep -A 10 VGA

This shows the kernel driver currently bound to the device. Look for the 'Kernel driver in use:' line — for AMD cards this will typically show 'amdgpu'.
